description: When output_path=None and Gemini returns a different format, edit_image saves to corrected extension (e.g., .jpg instead of .png) but leaves the original file untouched. This silently changes behavior from 'overwrite original'. Consider deleting/renaming original on correction or documenting this explicitly.
title: edit_image silently leaves original when extension corrected

description:
Modify generate_image to return structured response when saving.

**Files:**
- Modify: src/banana_appeal/server.py
- Test: tests/test_server.py

**Changes to _generate_image_impl:**
- Add verbose: bool = False parameter

title: Update generate_image with structured response

description:
Automate versioning and PyPI publishing based on conventional commits.

## Scope
- Configure python-semantic-release for automatic version bumping
- Add GitHub Actions workflow triggered on main branch merges
- Set up PyPI trusted publishing (OIDC) instead of API tokens
- Ensure conventional commit messages drive version increments (feat→minor, fix→patch, breaking→major)

## Notes
- Project already uses conventional commits per AGENTS.md
- Build job already validates package with twine check
title: Set up semantic release and PyPI publishing

description:
Run complete test suite, linting, and type checking. Fix any issues.

**Commands:**
```bash
uv run pytest -v
uv run ruff check .
uv run ruff format .
uv run pyrefly check src/banana_appeal/
```

**Fix any failures or linting errors found.**
title: Run full test suite and fix issues

description: start_time is set before image loading at line 604, not right before API call like other tools. This inflates timing to include file I/O instead of just API call time.
title: Fix generation timing in blend_images includes image loading

description:
Document the new structured response format and verbose parameter.

**Update README.md or docs/ with:**
- New response format examples
- verbose parameter usage
- Extension auto-correction behavior
- Example responses (default vs verbose)

**Keep it brief - focus on usage examples.**
title: Update documentation for structured responses

description: The _correct_extension function has duplicate logic for determining new_ext (lines 199 and 209). Extract the canonical extension derivation into a single expression at the start. Estimated reduction: ~20 lines to ~16 lines.
title: Simplify _correct_extension by extracting canonical extension logic

description: The comparison 'corrected_path \!= request.image_path' compares Path objects directly. Using .resolve() would be more robust against symlinks or unusual path representations. Low priority since both paths come from the same base path in practice.
title: Use resolved paths in edit_image extension correction comparison
Test auto-commit

description: The Gemini API supports image upscaling via the UpscaleImageConfig. Add a new upscale_image tool that takes an image path and upscale factor (2x, 4x, 8x) and returns an enhanced, higher-resolution version.
title: Add upscale_image tool

description:
Add ImageDimensions Pydantic model to models.py for typed width/height.

**Files:**
- Modify: src/banana_appeal/models.py (add after ImageFormat class, ~line 28)
- Test: tests/test_models.py

**Implementation:**
```python
class ImageDimensions(BaseModel):
    """Image dimensions in pixels."""
    model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)
    width: int = Field(gt=0, description="Image width in pixels")
    height: int = Field(gt=0, description="Image height in pixels")
```

**Tests:**
- test_create_dimensions: Create valid dimensions
- test_dimensions_immutable: Verify frozen model

**TDD:** Write tests first, verify they fail, implement, verify pass, commit.
title: Add ImageDimensions model

description: Gemini 3 Pro Image supports Google Search grounding, which allows generating images with real-time, fact-verified information (e.g., current weather visualizations). Add an optional use_search parameter that enables tools=[{'google_search': {}}] in the config. Only works with gemini-3-pro-image-preview model.
title: Add Google Search grounding option for Gemini 3 Pro

description: blend_images loads images concurrently and appends to shared list as tasks complete, making order nondeterministic. Gemini blends are order-sensitive. Should preserve user-specified order by preallocating list and assigning by index.
title: blend_images has nondeterministic image order

description: verbose=True is accepted when no save_path/output_path provided, but inline Image responses drop all verbose metadata. This silently ignores the flag. Consider rejecting verbose when not saving, or returning structured metadata alongside inline image.
title: verbose=True silently ignored for inline responses

description: When verbose=True, _add_verbose_fields opens image bytes with Pillow to get dimensions. If Pillow fails (corrupt/unsupported), the MCP call aborts even though the image was already saved. Should catch decode errors and return base ImageOperationResponse with a warning instead.
title: Catch Pillow decode errors in verbose mode

description: _extract_image_data maps unknown MIME types to PNG. The common alias 'image/jpg' fails ImageFormat('jpg') validation and falls back to PNG, causing JPEG bytes to be mislabeled. Add alias handling to treat image/jpg as JPEG.
title: Handle image/jpg MIME alias in _extract_image_data

description:
Follow MCP naming conventions and better describe the project's purpose as a generic image generation MCP server.

Plan: docs/plans/2025-12-10-rename-to-depict-mcp.md

Execution order:
1. bd rename-prefix d- (before file changes)
2. mv src/banana_appeal src/depict_mcp
3. Update file contents (pyproject.toml, tests, docs, workflows)
4. uv sync --dev (regenerate lock file)
5. Verify: uv run pytest && uv run ruff check . && bd list --status=open
6. Commit with breaking change marker
title: Rename project from banana-appeal to depict-mcp

description: The edit_image function handles deletion failure gracefully (logs warning, continues), but there's no test verifying this behavior. If someone refactors this code, they might accidentally make deletion failure break the operation. Add a test that mocks AsyncPath.unlink to fail and verifies the operation still succeeds.
title: Add test for edit_image deletion failure scenario

description:
Allow specifying model (Flash vs Pro) per tool call instead of only via BANANA_MODEL env var.

**Use case:** Claude Code could automatically choose Flash for quick iterations and Pro for final high-quality diagrams/infographics.

**Implementation:**
1. Add optional 'model: str | None' parameter to GenerateImageRequest, EditImageRequest, BlendImagesRequest
2. Pass model parameter through to Gemini API client initialization
3. Update MCP tool definitions to expose the parameter
4. Document that None/unspecified uses BANANA_MODEL default

**Trade-offs:**
- Pros: More flexible, optimize cost/quality per request
- Cons: Adds complexity, could confuse which model is active
title: Add per-call model selection to MCP tools

description: The Gemini API supports configurable aspect ratios (1:1, 2:3, 3:2, 3:4, 4:3, 4:5, 5:4, 9:16, 16:9, 21:9) via ImageConfig. Currently we hardcode response_modalities but don't expose aspect_ratio control. Add an optional aspect_ratio parameter to generate_image tool.
title: Add aspect ratio parameter to generate_image

description: The three implementation functions (_generate_image_impl, _edit_image_impl, _blend_images_impl) share nearly identical response-building logic for: extracting image data, calling _correct_extension, building ImageOperationResponse, and optionally calling _add_verbose_fields. Extract to a reusable helper. Estimated reduction: ~40 lines.
title: Extract _build_image_response helper to reduce duplication

description: The pytest-asyncio package uses 'asyncio.get_event_loop_policy' which is deprecated and slated for removal in Python 3.16. Monitor upstream for fix. Warning appears in plugin.py at lines 772, 874, 1216.
title: Track pytest-asyncio deprecation warning for Python 3.16

description: Current default is gemini-2.0-flash-preview-image-generation. The gemini-2.5-flash-image model is newer and offers better performance. Consider also supporting gemini-3-pro-image-preview for advanced features (4K, text rendering, search grounding).
title: Update default model to gemini-2.5-flash-image

description: Refactoring opportunity identified during code review: return ImageDimensions directly instead of tuple unpacking, shorten docstring, remove intermediate variables. Reduces function from 54 to 32 lines (41% reduction) with no behavior change.
title: Simplify _add_verbose_fields function

description: The Gemini cookbook recommends chat mode for iterative image editing as it maintains context through 'thought signatures'. This enables better character consistency and natural editing workflows. Consider adding a new tool or mode that maintains conversation state across multiple edit requests.
title: Add chat/multi-turn mode for iterative editing

description: The Gemini API supports negative prompts to specify what should NOT appear in generated images. Add an optional negative_prompt parameter to generate_image to allow users to exclude unwanted elements.
title: Add negative_prompt parameter for exclusions

description: The Gemini API supports a seed parameter for reproducible image generation. This is useful when iterating on a prompt - you can keep the same seed to see how prompt changes affect output while keeping other randomness constant. Add an optional seed parameter to generate_image.
title: Add seed parameter for reproducible generation

description:
Currently using verbose=True/False for metadata. Add finer-grained control like include=['dimensions', 'timing'] to let callers specify exactly which fields to return, minimizing token usage.

Depends on: structured response implementation (in progress)

Example API:
```python
generate_image(prompt='...', include=['dimensions', 'size_bytes'])
```
title: Add field selection for metadata responses

description: The blend_images tool currently allows up to 14 images regardless of model. However, gemini-2.5-flash-image (nano-banana) only supports 3 images max, while gemini-3-pro-image-preview supports 14 (6 high-fidelity). The limit should be model-aware, or we should document/warn users about this limitation.
title: Fix blend_images max limit based on model

description: When edit_image extension correction triggers original file deletion but deletion fails, users only see the extension mismatch warning. They might be confused about why both files exist. Consider adding a warning like 'Note: original file at X could not be deleted' when deletion is attempted but fails.
title: Add warning when edit_image original file deletion fails

description: blend_images uses path comparison logic for original_path instead of warning-based logic like generate_image and edit_image. Should use: original_path=str(request.output_path) if warning else None
title: Fix original_path inconsistency in blend_images

description: The google-genai package uses '_UnionGenericAlias' which is deprecated and slated for removal in Python 3.17. Monitor upstream for fix or find workaround. Warning: .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/google/genai/types.py:43
title: Track google-genai deprecation warning for Python 3.17

description:
Add _add_verbose_fields async function to populate metadata.

**Files:**
- Modify: src/banana_appeal/server.py (add after _correct_extension)
- Test: tests/test_server.py

**Function signature:**
```python
async def _add_verbose_fields(
    response: ImageOperationResponse,
    image_data: bytes,
    generation_time_ms: float,
    model_name: str,
    seed: int | None,
) -> ImageOperationResponse:
```

**Implementation:**
- Use PIL.Image.open(BytesIO(data)).size for dimensions (header only, fast)
- Run in thread pool with to_thread.run_sync
- Return new response with verbose fields populated

**Tests:**
- test_adds_dimensions
- test_adds_size_bytes
- test_adds_generation_time
- test_adds_model_name
- test_adds_seed_when_provided
- test_preserves_base_fields

**Also update imports:** Add ImageDimensions, ImageOperationResponse to imports from models.

**TDD:** Write tests first, implement, commit.
title: Add verbose fields helper function

description:
Add _correct_extension function to server.py for auto-correcting file extensions.

**Files:**
- Modify: src/banana_appeal/server.py (add after _log_metrics, ~line 166)
- Test: tests/test_server.py

**Function signature:**
```python
def _correct_extension(save_path: Path, actual_format: ImageFormat) -> tuple[Path, str | None]:
```

**Logic:**
- JPEG accepts .jpg and .jpeg
- No extension → append correct one
- Mismatch → correct and return warning
- Match → return path unchanged, None warning

**Test cases:**
- .jpg/.jpeg for JPEG → no correction
- .png for PNG → no correction
- .json for JPEG → correct to .jpg
- .png for JPEG → correct to .jpg
- .JSON (uppercase) → normalize and correct
- no extension → append .jpg or .png

**Warning format:** 'Gemini returned {FORMAT} image; saved as {new_ext} (requested {old_ext})'

**TDD:** Write tests first, implement, commit.
title: Add extension correction function

description:
Check what additional metadata is available from Gemini API responses that we could expose:

- usage_metadata (token counts)
- C2PA signature details  
- Model version info
- Any other response fields we're not capturing

Add valuable fields to the structured response.
title: Investigate additional Gemini response metadata

description: Gemini supports 1K, 2K, and 4K output resolutions via ImageConfig.image_size. Add an optional resolution parameter (defaulting to 1K) to allow users to request higher quality outputs when needed. Note: resolution values must be uppercase (1K not 1k).
title: Add resolution parameter to generate_image

description:
Add ImageOperationResponse Pydantic model for structured responses.

**Files:**
- Modify: src/banana_appeal/models.py (add after ImageDimensions)
- Test: tests/test_models.py

**Model fields:**
- path: str | None (where image was saved)
- format: str (jpeg, png, etc.)
- warnings: list[str] (any corrections)
- original_path: str | None (if extension corrected)
- dimensions: ImageDimensions | None (verbose)
- size_bytes: int | None (verbose)
- generation_time_ms: float | None (verbose)
- model: str | None (verbose)
- seed: int | None (verbose)

**Tests:**
- test_minimal_response
- test_response_with_path
- test_response_with_correction
- test_verbose_response
- test_to_dict (exclude_none=True)

**TDD:** Write tests first, implement, commit.
title: Add ImageOperationResponse model
